headline: SILVER DEFICIT HITS YEAR SIX: 762 MILLION-OZ CUMULATIVE SHORTFALL AS 2026 GAP WIDENS 15%
snippet: The Silver Institute confirmed the market's sixth consecutive annual supply deficit, with the 2026 shortfall widening 15% to 46.3 million troy ounces—bringing the six-year cumulative gap to 762 million ounces. Industrial demand from solar panels, EVs, and AI data centres continues to outpace mine supply growth, confirming an era of structurally depleted silver stockpiles.

headline: GOLD STEADIES AT $4,636 AS CHINA LOGS 17TH STRAIGHT MONTHLY PURCHASE — J.P. MORGAN EYES $5,055 BY Q4
snippet: Gold settled at $4,636/oz on May 1, down $50 intraday but up $1,351 year-over-year. China's People's Bank reported its 17th consecutive monthly gold purchase in March, adding 5 tonnes to reach 2,313 tonnes (9% of total foreign reserves). J.P. Morgan Research forecasts gold will average $5,055/oz by Q4 2026.

headline: SILVER CLIMBS ABOVE $74 AS WEAK DOLLAR AND $715B AI CAPEX BOOM FUEL INDUSTRIAL DEMAND
snippet: Silver extended its rally above $74/oz on May 1, supported by a depreciating US dollar and record AI infrastructure spending—$715 billion committed by Meta, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon in the latest round of capex guidance. Silver has gained roughly 150% over the past year, driven by a rare convergence of industrial and investment demand.

headline: ABRASILVER DRILLS 68.5 METRES AT 343 G/T SILVER AT DIABLILLOS — TOP INTERVAL HITS 917 G/T
source_name: Junior Mining Network / AbraSilver Resource Corp.
snippet: AbraSilver Resource Corp. (TSX:ABRA) confirmed strong mineralization continuity between the Oculto and JAC zones at its Diablillos project in Argentina with a Phase VI intercept of 68.5m grading 343 g/t silver, including 17m at 917 g/t from 138m depth. Results point to further expansion potential at Oculto East and strengthen the case for an updated resource estimate.

headline: NGEX MINERALS FINDS NEW COPPER ZONE JUST 60M BELOW SURFACE AT LUNAHUASI — OPEN IN ALL DIRECTIONS
snippet: NGEx Minerals (TSX:NGEX) closed Phase 4 drilling at Lunahuasi in Argentina by discovering a new high-grade copper-gold-silver zone 300m east of the Mars zone, returning 23m at 4.71% CuEq from just 60m below surface. Phase 5 drilling is planned for Q3 2026 and construction of an exploration adit will begin, suggesting the system is much larger than currently defined.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD & SILVER HITS 1,392 G/T SILVER — FOURTH MAJOR DISCOVERY AT IDAHO'S GALENA MINE
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX:USA) identified six new high-grade silver-copper-antimony veins in the 43L-TJ Vein Complex at its Galena Mine in Idaho, its fourth major new discovery in recent months. Peak assays reached 3,714 g/t silver from zones located within 25m of existing underground infrastructure, pointing to near-term resource growth.

headline: COPPER CLIMBS 1% TO $5.98/LB — US 25% TARIFF DECISION DUE BY JUNE, 330,000-TONNE DEFICIT LOOMS
snippet: LME copper rose 1% to $5.98/lb on May 1 as physical supply tightness converged with policy risk: a US refined copper tariff of at least 25% is expected by June. J.P. Morgan projects a 330,000-tonne refined copper shortfall in 2026, underpinned by concentrate scarcity and limited new mine supply from top producers including Chile.

headline: VISIBLE GOLD MINES RAISES C$2.6M TO DRILL BESIDE IAMGOLD'S 11-MILLION-OZ NELLIGAN DEPOSIT IN QUEBEC
snippet: Visible Gold Mines (TSX-V) closed a C$2.6 million private placement to fund exploration at its Horsefly project in Quebec, directly adjacent to IAMGOLD's 11-million-ounce Nelligan Mining Complex. The raise sets up near-term drilling on one of Canada's highest-profile exploration footprints relative to a world-class, undeveloped gold deposit.

headline: GLOBAL MINING M&A HITS $21.6B IN Q1 2026 — STRONGEST START TO A YEAR SINCE 2023
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $21.6 billion across 121 transactions in Q1 2026, up 34% from Q1 2025, according to White & Case LLP. The surge extends 2025's record $93.7 billion full-year tally—the highest in 13 years—as critical mineral supply-chain priorities and energy-transition demand reshape deal flow in gold, copper, and battery metals.

headline: CRITICAL ELEMENTS LITHIUM DELIVERS PHASE 1 WINTER 2026 ASSAYS FROM ROSE WEST DISCOVERY IN QUEBEC
snippet: Critical Elements Lithium Corporation (TSX-V:CRE) released Phase 1 Winter 2026 drill assay results from the Rose West Discovery in Quebec, advancing one of Canada's most closely watched hard-rock lithium projects. Results add to a growing dataset supporting a formal resource estimate at a project in an increasingly strategic minerals province.

headline: MUSTANG ENERGY RECEIVES FIRST ASSAYS FROM 11 DRILL HOLES AT SURPRISE CREEK URANIUM-COPPER PROJECT, SASKATCHEWAN
snippet: Mustang Energy Corp. (CSE:MUST) received initial assay results from 11 diamond drill holes at its Surprise Creek Uranium-Copper project in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin. The data expands the geological picture at an address gaining attention as AI-driven power demand bolsters the long-term uranium supply outlook.
